text:
IMPARTIAL NEWS + INTELLIGENT DEBATE

Account

Trap shooter Nathan Hales securedTeam GB’sthird gold ofParis 2024as he broke the Olympic record on course to a barnstorming victory at Chateauroux.

Meanwhile, the men’s Olympic triathlon has been postponed at the last minute due to high levels of E. coli in the Seine, as a heatwave which threatens to reach 40°C hits the French capital.

And in the chlorinated pool, GB’s men’s 4x200m freestyle relay team retained their title in dominant fashion after blowing the rest of the competition out of the water.

The men’s triathlon has been moved to Wednesday due to concerns overwater qualityafter tests revealed some points of the course were “still above the acceptable limits”. Swimming training for the triathletes was cancelled on Sunday and Monday.

The men’s event is now set to be held on Wednesday at 9.45am UK time, after the women’s event, which is set to start at 8am.

On Monday, it was revealed Team GB swimmerAdam Peatyhad tested positive for Covid, after narrowly missing out on gold in the 100m breaststroke.

In a statement, Team GB said: “He is hopeful to be back in competition for the relay events later in the swimming programme. As in any case of illness, the situation is being managed appropriately, with all usual precautions being taken to keep the wider delegation healthy.”

This live blog is now closed.

Well, I think that’s enough drama for one day. I, for one, am exhausted, never mind the athletes! We’ll be back again tomorrow hopefully for more gold medal-related shenanigans.

Simone Biles was the headline act at Paris 2024. And she brought the house down by leading Team USA to an emphatic gold medal-winning performance in the women’s team gymnastics final.

Biles was last on stage, on the floor exercise, more or less needing to do only a couple of handstands and a cartwheel to clinch the Olympic gold medal for USA. They led from the first rotation – their victory was comprehensive.

And Biles closed the show with a phenomenal floor routine. She generously showed us she is human by stepping out of the area, but she still got the crowd on their feet.

Read my full analysis from the Bercy Arenahere.

A stunning picture showing a Brazilian surfer appearing to defy gravity has gone viral on social media.

Jerome Brouillet captured this incredible shot of Gabriel Medina celebrating after catching the perfect wave.

James Toney tells the story behindthe most iconic photograph of this summer’s Olympics.

The Fab Four collect their second Olympic gold in three years, to the delight of the thousands of Brits in the stadium.

They are so hard to beat in that formation: Duncan Scott’s last leg time would have been good enough to win the individual race by eight tenths of a second.

Unbelievable scenes. It’s not done yet. Two match points saved and four points in a row to finish it.

Murray and Evans out in the middle doing all manner of insane celebrations. Silly old sods. See you all again in the next round.

GREAT BRITAIN ARE BACK-TO-BACK CHAMPIONS!

Duncan Scott has seven Olympic medals and is Britain’s most successful Olympic swimmer of all-time, but a brilliant team effort.

The first three all swam sub 1:46s and then Scott brought it home with a 1:43.95 to hold off the Americans. A wire-to-wire win. Brilliant.

GB lead at the halfway mark, Matt Richards and then Duncan Scott to go…

Here come the Brits then. After resting this morning, it is the full strength line-up that won gold three years ago.

James Guy, Tom Dean, Matt Richards and bringing it home on the anchor leg Duncan Scott, who will become the most successful British swimmer of all-time if he wins any metal tonight, surpassing Adam Peaty on medal count.

We are into the final match tie-break. It wasn’t meant to be easy, remember. Murray and Evans lost the second set tie-break 10-8. Straight to it now, Andy. I have a train to catch.

8-8, you know the score.

It was never going to be easy, was it?

Daniel Wiffen has picked up his gold medal and quite rightly is milking every second of it, sprinting around the stadium high-fiving fans, hugging anyone and everyone, but the biggest embrace of all is reserved for the man who can only be his twin brother and training partner Nathan. Hugely emotional scenes in La Defense Arena.

The men’s 200m breaststroke semi-finals are next, and then it’s the relay final with Team GB going for gold at 9.15pm.

Tie-break in the second set. Win this and the Murray goodbye gets a little longer.

Alas, Anna Hopkin. She misses the Olympic final by a distance in the end, 0.56 seconds, an age in 100m freestyle swimming. A bit of a disappointment having finished seventh in Tokyo, but the 50m free is arguably her better event. She will go again on Saturday.

Main takeaway from the USA gymnastics team press conference after their women’s gold medal win: Simon Biles is having fun again. And they have a name for their team which contains an F-word.

There goes Anna Hopkin in the women’s 100m freestyle semi-final. Like Matt Richards, she has managed sixth place and like Matt Richards, that probably won’t be enough to get her through. But we wait in hope.

4-4 in the second and still no break. Murray has more resolve than me. Nails gone.

Three games all in the second set. On serve. The crucial seventh might be a good time to snap the trend with the ball in Belgian hands.

Before this week, only one Irish person had ever won any sort of Olympic medal in the swimming pool. After Mona McSharry’s bronze in the 100m breaststroke, Daniel Wiffen has made it three.

It’s an Olympic record too for the 2024 world champion, who took the lead at the halfway mark but then lost it to Italy’s Gregorio Paltrinieri with 150m to go.

But Wiffen, who trains alongside his twin brother Nathan at Loughborough University, had something left in the tank and made up an entire second in the final 50m to overhaul the Italian, who faded into third. 2020 Olympic champion Bobby Finke of the USA took silver.

WHAT A SWIM! DANIEL WIFFEN HAS WON GOLD FOR IRELAND!

Impartial news + intelligent debate

title: Team GB defend swimming relay gold as Murray and Evans reach quarter-finals
author: George Simms, Evan Bartlett, Tom Ward
description: Follow's i's live blog from day four of the Olympic Games in Paris
